name: Hydroponics & Agriculture
description: The ship's green decks — grow-lamps, soil beds, and seed vaults. Well-tended, they turn a rationed voyage into a fed one.

tiers.flavor: New grow-lamps and seed stock go in; the green decks come back brighter, and the galley plans a little less anxiously.
The hydroponics are rebuilt across a second ring of decks — the ship now grows more than it strictly must, and the surplus tastes like security.
The agriculture decks reach their fullest form: closed-loop, self-seeding, abundant. The ship will not go hungry while these decks turn.

name: Schools & Archives
description: Classrooms, libraries, and the rites that carry a craft from a dying hand to a young one. The subsystem that keeps every other subsystem knowable.

tiers.flavor: New classrooms open and the archives are recopied; a little more of what the founders knew survives another generation.
The schools are rebuilt into a true academy — apprenticeships, examinations, a craft passed hand to hand on purpose rather than by luck.
The archive becomes the ship's deep memory, redundant and revered; nothing the founders understood need ever be wholly lost again.

name: Engineering Bay
description: Machine shops, spare-part fabricators, and the diagnostic rigs that catch a fault before it becomes a hull breach.

tiers.flavor: New tooling and a proper machine shop go in; the engineers stop improvising and start building.
The engineering bay is rebuilt into a full fabrication hall — the ship can now make the parts it once could only ration.
The bay reaches its pinnacle: foundry, mill, and clean room together, able to remanufacture nearly anything the ship is made of.

name: Life Support & Habitat
description: Scrubbers, recyclers, and the sealed habitats that stand between the crew and the cold. A stronger habitat wears its life support down slower.

tiers.flavor: The scrubbers and circulators are overhauled; the air comes easier and the decks feel less like a machine and more like a home.
Life support is rebuilt with a second independent loop — a failure that would once have emptied a deck now merely inconveniences it.
The habitat reaches its fullest: triple-redundant air, water, and warmth. The ship breathes as steadily as a world.

name: Medical Bay
description: Surgeries, isolation wards, and gene-clinics. A well-equipped medical deck turns a plague into a scare.

tiers.flavor: New surgical kit and clean fields go in; the medics stop losing patients they should have saved.
The medical bay is rebuilt into a true hospital — wards, a proper theatre, and a pharmacy the ship can actually stock.
The bay reaches its pinnacle: gene-clinic, surgical suite, and long-term wards together, a medicine equal to a century in the dark.

name: Security & Peacekeeping
description: Peacekeepers, brig, and the councils that keep a fractious ship from turning on itself. Strong security cools a standoff before it burns.

tiers.flavor: The watch is re-equipped and retrained; the corridors feel safer without feeling watched.
Security is rebuilt into a real peacekeeping service — mediation, patrol, and a jail the ship hopes never to fill.
The peacekeepers reach their fullest form: trusted, restrained, and capable — a force that keeps the peace mostly by being ready to.
